vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. P1 (String of Nickels) and P2 (Mexican Mint) are absent or completely desiccated/lost. P3 (Pothos) is the only surviving specimen, though it is in a state of extreme senescence. P4 (Silver Guest) is not visually identifiable as a distinct plant, likely lost.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty soil, no visible plant matter.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Empty soil, no visible plant matter.
plant_audit.P3_Pothos_Black_Pot: Single remaining leaf, chlorotic and drooping. Stem is thin and lacks turgor pressure.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3; white paper/receipt debris present on desk surface.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows P3 with a single leaf and P1/P2 pots empty.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t3: Introduction of a small electronic component (IC/chip) on the desk surface.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Electronic component remains; no change in plant health.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_t1: Significant drop in ambient light, obscuring details.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Return to baseline lighting; P3 remains in a state of terminal decline.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is failing. The lack of foliage in P1 and P2 indicates total mortality. The Pothos (P3) is suffering from extreme nutrient deficiency or root system failure, evidenced by the singular, pale leaf and lack of new growth.
anomalies: Introduction of electronic component (IC/chip) on desk surface.
Presence of eggshell fragments in P3 pot (likely a failed soil amendment attempt).
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is in a state of collapse. The registry-defined plants P1, P2, and P4 are no longer present. The sole survivor, P3, is a Pothos specimen that is severely stunted and likely terminal. The presence of non-organic debris (receipts, electronic components) suggests the desk is being used for storage rather than active cultivation, contributing to the neglect of the specimens.
